search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG109 transient loss consciousness event witness history ECG red flags transfer records 2023
site:dha.gov.ae syncope emergency clinical guideline UAE
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic vasovagal, situational, cardiac or epileptic diagnosis
automatic ECG interpretation, risk score, referral, admission or discharge
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The exact source requires a structured assessment before a diagnosis.
ECG interpretation, risk and disposition remain qualified clinician actions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness; paediatric syncope and non-TLoC falls require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Initial and emergency assessment of suspected transient loss of consciousness.
UAE_applicability: Assessment domains transfer, but UAE ambulance, ECG, cardiology, neurology, emergency referral and driving-law pathways govern.
recency_verification: NICE CG109 last updated 2023-11-21 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact event-history section supports onset, event duration, severity or injury impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient or witness concerns for suspected transient loss of consciousness without diagnosing syncope.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording what occurred before, during and after the event, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, injury, duration, confusion and focal weakness from patient and witness accounts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact initial-assessment section supports clinician risk context, measured vital signs, focused cardiovascular or neurological examination, severity, appearance and existing ECG or test results only when assessed or reviewed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports prior-event, medical, family and medicine history, measured vital signs, cardiovascular and neurological signs, ECG status and clinician-selected testing when an underlying problem is suspected.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact record-transfer section supports recording existing ECG or report transfer, clinician documentation plan and arranged handover without selecting a destination or diagnosis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports careful recording of all accounts and transfer of paramedic, ECG and patient-report records to the receiving clinician.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact urgent-feature section supports recording time-critical findings, clinician-decided disposition and completed specialist discussion; it does not generate an urgency conclusion.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded urgent-risk context including ECG abnormalities, heart failure signs, exertional events, family history, unexplained breathlessness and murmur without generating urgency or referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact event circumstances, witness account, posture, prodrome, movements, recovery, family history, medication context, orthostatic measurements, ECG findings, diagnosis, risk score and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
